response:
SOME EXPERTS PREDICT THAT MOORE'S LAW WILL CEASE TO HOLD AROUND THE EARLY 2020S DUE TO PHYSICAL LIMITS IN TRANSISTOR SCALING AND QUANTUM TUNNELING EFFECTS THAT PREVENT FURTHER SHRINKAGE OF SILICON COMPONENTS.
***
OTHER ANALYSTS ARGUE THAT THE LAW WILL NOT TRULY END BUT WILL EVOLVE AS THE INDUSTRY TRANSITIONS TO NEW MATERIALS LIKE GRAPHENE OR THREE DIMENSIONAL CHIP ARCHITECTURES THAT SUSTAIN PERFORMANCE GAINS WITHOUT RELYING SOLELY ON DENSITY INCREASES.
SOME
